tickets.body:
## Page

`nist_phase54_page_0046`

## Scope

Owner: `pdf_oxide_core`

This is a page-level tracker for one focused core extraction defect: nested list segmentation under AC-2 Account Management.

## Source artifacts

- Page image: `artifacts/pdf_lab/project_agent_hardening/fresh_next30_agentic_second_pass_20260601Tnow_final_full_rerun_v2/pages/page_0046/page.png`
- Overlay image: `artifacts/pdf_lab/project_agent_hardening/fresh_next30_agentic_second_pass_20260601Tnow_final_full_rerun_v2/pages/page_0046/bbox_overlay_current.png`
- Current extraction: `artifacts/pdf_lab/project_agent_hardening/fresh_next30_agentic_second_pass_20260601Tnow_final_full_rerun_v2/pages/page_0046/release_extraction_blocks.json`
- Model review: `artifacts/pdf_lab/project_agent_hardening/fresh_next30_agentic_second_pass_20260601Tnow_final_full_rerun_v2/pages/page_0046/model_review.json`
- Selection summary: `artifacts/pdf_lab/page46_selection_20260604/selection_summary.md`

## Bug

Current extraction merges item `h.` and its numbered child items into one list block:

- `actual:p46:block:18`

Extracted text:

```text
h. Notify account managers and [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles] within: 1. [Assignment: organization-defined time period] when accounts are no longer required; 2. [Assignment: organization-defined time period] when users are terminated or transferred; and 3. [Assignment: organization-defined time period] when system usage or need-to-know changes for an individual;
```

Expected extraction shape:

- one parent list item for `h. Notify account managers ... within:`
- one child list item for `1. [Assignment: organization-defined time period] when accounts are no longer required;`
- one child list item for `2. [Assignment: organization-defined time period] when users are terminated or transferred; and`
- one child list item for `3. [Assignment: organization-defined time period] when system usage or need-to-know changes for an individual;`

Nearby expected examples on the same page:

- `actual:p46:block:11` parent `d. Specify:`
- `actual:p46:block:12`, `13`, `14` numbered children
- `actual:p46:block:19` parent `i. Authorize access to the system based on:`
- `actual:p46:block:20`, `21`, `22` numbered children

## Checklist

- [ ] Add a focused page-46 regression that proves `h.` is split into parent plus numbered child list blocks.
- [ ] Patch core list segmentation/materialization so an alphabetic item ending with `within:` does not absorb indented numbered children.
- [ ] Verify the page-46 extraction artifact after the patch.
- [ ] Verify nearby `d.` and `i.` nested list structures do not regress.

## Stop condition

Focused test plus extraction artifact prove the `h.` nested list is split correctly and same-page known-good nested lists remain correct.
tickets.title: [PDF Lab] nist_phase54_page_0046: nested list segmentation under AC-2 h

tickets.body:
## Page

- Project: `nist-phase54-toc-backed`
- Page: `nist_phase54_page_0045`
- Label: NIST p.45 / printed p.18 / `3.1 ACCESS CONTROL`
- Human annotation artifact: `artifacts/pdf_lab/page45_human_reannotation_20260604/in_progress_entry.json`
- PDF Lab UI route: `http://localhost:3002/#pdf-lab/labeling`

## Intent

Use this one annotated page as the tracker for deliberate `pdf_oxide` repair. Raw human-vs-agent diffs are evidence, not standalone bugs. Fix one checklist item at a time with a page-bound regression and extraction proof.

## Bug List

- [x] Core: AC-1 control list is over-segmented. The nested `a/b/c`, `1/2`, and `(a)/(b)` content should materialize as one semantic list region (`p45_control_list_merged`), not many separate list/prose fragments.
  - Evidence: `artifacts/pdf_lab/page45_issue2_item1_core_list_20260604/proof_summary.json` shows before list-like count `8`, after list-like count `1`, after list ID `actual:p45:block:10`, and `Related Controls` not absorbed.
  - Focused command: `cargo test block_classifier --lib` -> `49 passed; 0 failed; 4498 filtered out`.
  - Page extraction command: `PYTHONPATH=python .venv/bin/python scripts/pdf_lab/snapshot_current_extraction.py --pdf /home/graham/workspace/experiments/pi-mono/packages/ux-lab/public/NIST_SP_800-53r5.pdf --out artifacts/pdf_lab/page45_issue2_item1_core_list_20260604/after_snapshot.json --max-pages 45 --apply-mode release --ledger python/pdf_oxide/presets/document_families/nist_sp_800_53r5_promotion_ledger.json` -> exit 0.
- [x] NIST preset: `Control Enhancements: None.` needs a NIST-specific semantic label such as `labeled_enhancements`, not generic `labeled_paragraph`.
  - Evidence: `artifacts/pdf_lab/page45_issue2_item2_labeled_enhancements_20260604/proof_summary.json` shows `ok: true`; matching block `actual:p45:block:13` has `type: labeled_enhancements` and `semantic_role: nist_control_enhancements_none`.
  - Focused command: `PYTHONPATH=python .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_nist_control_heading_preset.py::test_nist_control_enhancements_none_is_labeled_enhancements tests/test_nist_control_heading_preset.py::test_nist_control_and_enhancement_headings_are_section_headings` -> `2 passed in 0.13s`.
  - Page extraction command: `PYTHONPATH=python .venv/bin/python scripts/pdf_lab/snapshot_current_extraction.py --pdf /home/graham/workspace/experiments/pi-mono/packages/ux-lab/public/NIST_SP_800-53r5.pdf --out artifacts/pdf_lab/page45_issue2_item2_labeled_enhancements_20260604/after_snapshot.json --max-pages 45 --apply-mode release --ledger python/pdf_oxide/presets/document_families/nist_sp_800_53r5_promotion_ledger.json` -> exit 0.
- [x] NIST preset: visible quick-link text (`Quick link to Access Control Summary Table`) should classify as `section_link`.
  - Evidence: `artifacts/pdf_lab/page45_issue2_item3_section_link_20260604/proof_summary.json` shows `ok: true`; matching block `actual:p45:block:7` has `type: section_link` and `semantic_role: nist_quick_link`.
  - Focused command: `PYTHONPATH=python .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_nist_control_heading_preset.py::test_nist_quick_link_is_section_link tests/test_nist_control_heading_preset.py::test_nist_control_enhancements_none_is_labeled_enhancements tests/test_nist_control_heading_preset.py::test_nist_control_and_enhancement_headings_are_section_headings` -> `3 passed in 0.13s`.
  - Page extraction command: `PYTHONPATH=python .venv/bin/python scripts/pdf_lab/snapshot_current_extraction.py --pdf /home/graham/workspace/experiments/pi-mono/packages/ux-lab/public/NIST_SP_800-53r5.pdf --out artifacts/pdf_lab/page45_issue2_item3_section_link_20260604/after_snapshot.json --max-pages 45 --apply-mode release --ledger python/pdf_oxide/presets/document_families/nist_sp_800_53r5_promotion_ledger.json` -> exit 0.
- [x] NIST preset: `AC-1 POLICY AND PROCEDURES` should be one control heading, not split/duplicated as separate `AC-1` and title regions.
  - Evidence: `artifacts/pdf_lab/page45_issue2_item4_ac1_heading_20260604/proof_summary.json` shows `ok: true`, `normalized_matching_block_count: 1`, and the only matching block is `actual:p45:block:8` with `type: section_heading`, `semantic_role: nist_control_heading`, text `AC-1 POLICY AND PROCEDURES`.
  - Focused command: `PYTHONPATH=python .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_nist_control_heading_preset.py::test_nist_control_and_enhancement_headings_are_section_headings tests/test_nist_control_heading_preset.py::test_nist_quick_link_is_section_link tests/test_nist_control_heading_preset.py::test_nist_control_enhancements_none_is_labeled_enhancements` -> `3 passed in 0.13s`.
  - Page extraction evidence reused current item-3 snapshot: `artifacts/pdf_lab/page45_issue2_item3_section_link_20260604/after_snapshot.json`, generated by the page extraction command above -> exit 0.
- [x] NIST preset/export: every corrected region should preserve TOC lineage: `toc:0014 CHAPTER THREE THE CONTROLS` -> `toc:0015 3.1 ACCESS CONTROL`.
  - Evidence: `artifacts/pdf_lab/page45_issue2_item5_toc_lineage_20260604/proof_summary.json` shows `ok: true`; checked blocks `actual:p45:block:7`, `actual:p45:block:8`, `actual:p45:block:10`, and `actual:p45:block:13` all have `toc_path: ["toc:0014", "toc:0015"]` and breadcrumb `CHAPTER THREE THE CONTROLS -> 3.1 ACCESS CONTROL`.
  - Focused command: `PYTHONPATH=python .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_pdf_lab_snapshot_current_extraction.py::test_nist_page45_snapshot_adds_toc_lineage tests/test_pdf_lab_snapshot_current_extraction.py::test_toc_lineage_helper_ignores_unmapped_pdf_page tests/test_nist_control_heading_preset.py::test_nist_control_and_enhancement_headings_are_section_headings tests/test_nist_control_heading_preset.py::test_nist_quick_link_is_section_link tests/test_nist_control_heading_preset.py::test_nist_control_enhancements_none_is_labeled_enhancements` -> `5 passed in 0.15s`.
  - Page extraction command: `PYTHONPATH=python .venv/bin/python scripts/pdf_lab/snapshot_current_extraction.py --pdf /home/graham/workspace/experiments/pi-mono/packages/ux-lab/public/NIST_SP_800-53r5.pdf --out artifacts/pdf_lab/page45_issue2_item5_toc_lineage_20260604/after_snapshot.json --max-pages 45 --apply-mode release --ledger python/pdf_oxide/presets/document_families/nist_sp_800_53r5_promotion_ledger.json` -> exit 0.
- [x] NIST preset: running header, left DOI/sidebar text, and footer/page text must remain `page_chrome_noise` and not pollute body/list/reference extraction.
  - Evidence: `artifacts/pdf_lab/page45_issue2_item6_chrome_noise_20260604/proof_summary.json` shows `ok: true`, top-level block count `13`, and top-level non-chrome anchor query result `[]`.
  - Chrome blocks retained at top level: `actual:p45:block:0`, `actual:p45:block:1`, `actual:p45:block:2`, and `actual:p45:rotated_side_chrome:1`, all with `type: header_footer_noise`.
  - Note: fragment IDs `actual:p45:block:3`, `actual:p45:block:4`, and `actual:p45:block:5` remain only under `actual:p45:rotated_side_chrome:1.raw.fragments` as provenance; they are not top-level extracted blocks.
  - Focused command: `PYTHONPATH=python .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_pdf_lab_snapshot_current_extraction.py::test_rotated_side_chrome_suppresses_duplicate_margin_fragments tests/test_pdf_lab_snapshot_current_extraction.py::test_nist_page45_snapshot_adds_toc_lineage tests/test_pdf_lab_snapshot_current_extraction.py::test_toc_lineage_helper_ignores_unmapped_pdf_page tests/test_nist_control_heading_preset.py::test_nist_control_and_enhancement_headings_are_section_headings tests/test_nist_control_heading_preset.py::test_nist_quick_link_is_section_link tests/test_nist_control_heading_preset.py::test_nist_control_enhancements_none_is_labeled_enhancements` -> `6 passed in 0.14s`.
  - Page extraction command: `PYTHONPATH=python .venv/bin/python scripts/pdf_lab/snapshot_current_extraction.py --pdf /home/graham/workspace/experiments/pi-mono/packages/ux-lab/public/NIST_SP_800-53r5.pdf --out artifacts/pdf_lab/page45_issue2_item6_chrome_noise_20260604/after_snapshot.json --max-pages 45 --apply-mode release --ledger python/pdf_oxide/presets/document_families/nist_sp_800_53r5_promotion_ledger.json` -> exit 0.
  - Top-level non-chrome anchor query: `jq '[.pages[].blocks[] | select((.id? // "") | test("^actual:p45:")) | select(((.text? // "") | test("This publication is available|https://doi.org|NIST SP 800-53|CHAPTER THREE|PAGE 18|________")) and (.type? != "header_footer_noise")) | {id,type,source_type,semantic_role,text}]' artifacts/pdf_lab/page45_issue2_item6_chrome_noise_20260604/after_snapshot.json` -> `[]`.

## Current Human Annotation Summary

- Region count: 12
- Merged list region: `p45_control_list_merged`
- Current enhancement region: `p45_control_enhancements` with `family: labeled_paragraph`, `lead_label: Control Enhancements:`
- Section link region: `p45_quick_link` with `family: section_link`
- Control heading region: `p45_control_heading_ac1` with label `AC-1 POLICY AND PROCEDURES`
- Chrome regions: `p45_chr_header`, `p45_chr_sidebar`, `p45_chr_footer`
- Missing TOC lineage in human artifact: none

## Definition of Done

For each checklist item fixed, add or update a focused regression tied to this page/fixture, run the focused command, and produce a current extraction artifact showing the corrected region behavior. Do not close this issue from broad test counts alone.
tickets.title: [PDF Lab] nist_phase54_page_0045: page-level annotation bugs
